. worship me: or, fall down before me
. to speak: or, to say that they knew him to be Christ
unto the sick of the palsy
. that: or, shall be perfected as his master
. rejected: or, frustrated against: or, within themselves
they that sat at meat with him
as soon as it was sprung up
. seemeth: or, thinketh that he hath
he that was possessed of the devils
him. many ancient copies add these words at the beginning of verse, and
, he passed by on the other side
on him, and passed by on the other side
. day by day: or, for the day
? in: or, out of his way
. the bright: Gr. a candle by its bright shining
of such things as ye have
. of: or, as you are able
? thy: Gr. do they require thy soul
is he that layeth up treasure
. neither: or, live not in careful suspense
of them that sit at meat
of them that sat at meat with him
it? pieces: drachma, here translated a piece of silver, is the eighth part of an ounce, which cometh to seven pence halfpenny, and is equal to the Roman penny
. measures: the word Batus in the original containeth nine gallons three quarts
. measures: the word here interpreted a measure in the original containeth about fourteen bushels and two quarts
tree, Be thou plucked up by the root
. this verse is not found in most of the Greek copies
to this end, that men ought
: in a city: Gr. in a certain city
. pounds: mina, here translated a pound, is twelve ounces and an half: which according to five shillings the ounce is three pounds two shillings and sixpence
they which shall be accounted worthy
. and the time: or, and, The time
shall they cause to be put to death
those things which are coming on
: With desire: or, I have heartily desired
. willing, remove: Gr. willing to remove
. Calvary: or, The place of a skull
? the living: or, him that liveth
at that which was come to pass
the things which are come to pass
. vanished: or, ceased to be seen of them
